title: E9-MT-065-FemsInteropProofSuite

scope.summary:
PROOF MT (no app code changes; proof only). GOAL: deliver a runtime proof suite that proves the native editor <-> FEMS (Pillar 12 typed-memory system, the project's typed-memory edge described as Pillar 12) interop works end-to-end against a REAL PostgreSQL database and the REAL EventLedger — no mocks, no stubs, no in-memory fakes, no SQLite. The suite proves three load-bearing interop claims: (1) a MemoryPack request issued from a document/editor context returns provenance-linked memory items that the native 'Relevant Memory' panel actually renders; (2) a 'Propose to Memory' action from the editor creates a review-gated proposal row in PostgreSQL AND emits an FR-EVT-MEM-001 event into the live EventLedger; (3) every FEMS editor action (open the Relevant Memory panel, trigger a MemoryPack refresh, invoke 'Propose to Memory', confirm/cancel the proposal) is reachable purely via AccessKit ids so an out-of-process swarm agent can drive the entire flow without screen-scraping or synthetic key events.

WHAT THIS MT IS NOT: it does NOT implement the FEMS panel, the MemoryPack client, the proposal action, or the AccessKit registration — those are delivered by MT-063 (FEMS Relevant Memory panel + MemoryPack client), MT-064 ('Propose to Memory' action + proposal lifecycle), and MT-041 (editor actions exposed through AccessKit). This MT ONLY writes the runtime proof suite that exercises and asserts that already-built behavior. Do not add features. Do not edit any non-test source file. If a needed behavior or backend API is missing, do NOT implement it here — record it as a typed blocker (see implementation_notes) and fail the affected proof with a descriptive message, never a silent skip.

EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE: a single new test file:
  CREATE  src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_fems_interop_proofs.rs  (~350-550 lines)
    - one Rust integration-test module that boots the native editor shell in an egui_kittest harness (the in-process UI test harness from the WP-011 toolkit), connected to a REAL handshake_core backend talking to a REAL PostgreSQL instance and the REAL EventLedger.
    - four #[test] (or #[tokio::test] if the harness is async) proof functions: proof_fems_01_memorypack_render, proof_fems_02_propose_creates_proposal_and_event, proof_fems_03_swarm_drives_fems_via_accesskit, proof_fems_04_procedural_proposal_stays_review_gated.
    - shared test fixtures at the top of the file: a fn that resolves the live PostgreSQL DSN and EventLedger endpoint from the standard test-config surface (env vars / workspace test config — see implementation_notes), a fn that creates a throwaway workspace_id + a seed document with known content, and a teardown that removes only the rows this test created (never TRUNCATE shared tables).

HOW TO BUILD THE SUITE (step-by-step, no-context implementable):
  1. Reuse WP-011 shell primitives — do NOT re-create them. Get the pane registry (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs), the command bus (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs), and the AccessKit id registry (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs) from the WP-011 shell (the same surfaces MT-031 wired the editor panes into and MT-041 registered actions into). Construct the kittest app exactly the way MT-041's harness (src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_e7_editor_action_accesskit.rs) constructs it, then additionally mount the FEMS Relevant Memory panel from MT-063 and enable the 'Propose to Memory' action from MT-064. Treat MT-041's harness as the canonical pattern for: building the egui_kittest app, advancing frames, querying the AccessKit tree by author_id, and dispatching AccessKit actions (ctx.perform_accesskit_action(node_id, action_id)).
  2. Real PostgreSQL only (HARD CONSTRAINT). The backend under test MUST be configured to use PostgreSQL + the EventLedger. There must be NO SQLite anywhere in this suite or its configuration. Resolve the live DSN from the project's standard integration-test config (env var such as HANDSHAKE_TEST_PG_DSN or the workspace test config file — confirm the exact key against the E6/backend-wiring MTs and existing integration tests before hardcoding). If the DSN is absent, the proof MUST fail with a clear 'live PostgreSQL DSN not configured' message — never silently fall back to an in-memory or SQLite store, and never pass green on an absent backend.
  3. proof_fems_01_memorypack_render: create a throwaway workspace and a seed document whose content deterministically matches at least one memory item already present (or seeded by the fixture) so a MemoryPack request returns at least one hit. Open the editor on that document, open the FEMS Relevant Memory panel via its AccessKit action, trigger the MemoryPack request (the panel issues GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/pack against the live backend), advance frames until the response renders, then ASSERT: (a) the HTTP/IPC call hit the real backend (observable via the returned payload referencing real PG row ids, not a fixture constant), (b) the panel's AccessKit subtree contains >= 1 rendered memory item node, and (c) each rendered item carries provenance linkage (source id / origin reference) — assert the provenance field is present and non-empty on at least one item. No mocked response.
  4. proof_fems_02_propose_creates_proposal_and_event: with the same live workspace, select editor content and invoke the 'Propose to Memory' action (MT-064) via its AccessKit id. After the action completes, ASSERT against the LIVE backend, not the UI alone: (a) a new proposal row exists in PostgreSQL for this workspace_id (query via the backend's proposals read API — GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals — and assert the new proposal id is present), and (b) an FR-EVT-MEM-001 event was emitted into the live EventLedger (query GET /api/flight_recorder, filter for event type FR-EVT-MEM-001 correlated to this workspace_id / proposal id, assert exactly the expected event appears). Both the proposal row and the ledger event must reference the same proposal identity so the proof shows the write and the event are correlated, not coincidental.
  5. proof_fems_03_swarm_drives_fems_via_accesskit: prove the swarm path. Drive the ENTIRE FEMS flow purely through AccessKit ids as an out-of-process agent would: discover the FEMS panel-open action, the MemoryPack-refresh action, the 'Propose to Memory' action, and the proposal confirm/cancel actions by author_id only (no direct Rust calls into the panel, no synthetic key events, no screen-scraping). For each step, look up the node by its stable author_id (per MT-041's 'editor.<pane>.<action>' convention extended for FEMS, e.g. editor.fems.panel-open, editor.fems.memorypack-refresh, editor.fems.propose, editor.fems.proposal-confirm), dispatch the AccessKit activate action, advance a frame, and assert the resulting state. ASSERT that the full sequence (open panel -> refresh MemoryPack -> propose -> reach review-gated proposal) is completable using only AccessKit ids, and that every targeted id is stable (deterministic, no random segments) so a stored swarm reference stays valid across frames.
  6. proof_fems_04_procedural_proposal_stays_review_gated: prove the safety invariant. A 'Propose to Memory' action — even one triggered procedurally / by an agent in proof_fems_03 — must create a REVIEW-GATED proposal and must NOT auto-commit into memory. After triggering the propose action end-to-end, ASSERT against the live backend that: (a) the proposal's status is the review-gated/pending state (not committed/applied), (b) no corresponding committed memory item was created as a side effect (query the live memory store and assert the proposed content did NOT appear as an active memory item without an explicit review/confirm), and (c) the FR-EVT-MEM-001 event records the proposal as pending review, not as an applied write. This proof is the regression guard for the typed-memory edge: it must fail loudly if any path lets a procedural proposal bypass the human/agent review gate.

DEPENDENCIES / UNBLOCK CONDITIONS:
  - MT-063 COMPLETE: the native FEMS 'Relevant Memory' panel and the MemoryPack client (GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/pack) exist and render provenance-linked items.
  - MT-064 COMPLETE: the 'Propose to Memory' editor action exists, creates a review-gated proposal via POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals, and emits FR-EVT-MEM-001.
  - MT-041 COMPLETE: editor actions (and, by extension, the FEMS actions) are exposed through the WP-011 AccessKit surface with stable author_ids; this MT reuses MT-041's kittest harness pattern and AccessKit id/action conventions.
  - A live PostgreSQL + EventLedger backend reachable from the test environment, configured via the project's standard integration-test config. No SQLite.

RISK IF MISSED: FEMS interop is asserted but never proven; the typed-memory edge (Pillar 12) could regress silently — a broken MemoryPack render, a proposal that auto-commits past the review gate, a missing FR-EVT-MEM-001 event, or an AccessKit id drift that breaks the swarm path — all without any failing test. This MT is the E9 cluster's end-to-end interop guarantee for the editor<->FEMS edge.
acceptance_criteria: AC-065-01: `cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_fems_interop_proofs` runs all four proofs against a REAL PostgreSQL + EventLedger backend and passes with zero failures. No SQLite, no mock backend, no in-memory store anywhere in the suite or its config.

AC-065-02 (FEMS-01): proof_fems_01_memorypack_render asserts that a MemoryPack request issued from a document context returns >= 1 provenance-linked item AND that the native Relevant Memory panel renders it (>= 1 memory-item node present in the panel's AccessKit subtree, with a non-empty provenance/source reference on at least one item). The response must come from the live backend, not a fixture constant.

AC-065-03 (FEMS-02): proof_fems_02_propose_creates_proposal_and_event asserts that invoking 'Propose to Memory' creates a new proposal row in the live PostgreSQL store (visible via GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals) AND emits an FR-EVT-MEM-001 event into the live EventLedger (visible via GET /api/flight_recorder), with both referencing the same proposal identity.

AC-065-04 (FEMS-03): proof_fems_03_swarm_drives_fems_via_accesskit asserts that the full FEMS flow (open panel -> refresh MemoryPack -> propose -> reach review-gated proposal) is driveable purely via AccessKit ids by an out-of-process-agent code path, using only stable deterministic author_ids and AccessKit action dispatch — no direct panel calls, no synthetic key events, no screen-scraping.

AC-065-05 (FEMS-04): proof_fems_04_procedural_proposal_stays_review_gated asserts that a procedurally/agent-triggered proposal stays review-gated end-to-end: proposal status is pending/review (not committed), no committed memory item is created as a side effect, and the FR-EVT-MEM-001 event records a pending-review proposal — not an applied write.

AC-065-06: No app/product code is modified by this MT. The only changed file is src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_fems_interop_proofs.rs. Any missing behavior or backend API is recorded as a typed blocker and surfaces as a descriptive test failure, never a silent skip and never a new feature implemented in the test file.
AC-065-07: The suite reuses the WP-011 shell primitives (pane registry, command bus, AccessKit id registry) and MT-041's kittest harness pattern; it does not re-create shell or AccessKit glue.
AC-065-08: `cargo build -p handshake-native --tests` succeeds with zero new warnings introduced by this MT.
proof_targets: PROOF-065-A: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_fems_interop_proofs -- --nocapture 2>&1`; paste full stdout. All four proofs show 'ok'; zero 'FAILED' lines; log lines show the live PostgreSQL DSN was used (not SQLite, not a mock).

PROOF-065-B (FEMS-01): paste the panel AccessKit subtree dump showing >= 1 rendered memory-item node, and paste the MemoryPack response showing a non-empty provenance/source reference on at least one item.

PROOF-065-C (FEMS-02): paste the GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals response showing the newly created proposal row, AND the GET /api/flight_recorder excerpt showing the correlated FR-EVT-MEM-001 event with the matching proposal identity.

PROOF-065-D (FEMS-03): paste the ordered list of AccessKit author_ids the swarm code path dispatched (panel-open, memorypack-refresh, propose, proposal step) with the activate action for each, demonstrating the flow completes purely via AccessKit ids.

PROOF-065-E (FEMS-04): paste the proposal-status assertion (pending/review, not committed) and the live memory-store query proving no committed memory item was created as a side effect, plus the FR-EVT-MEM-001 event field showing pending-review.
implementation_notes: IN-065-01: PostgreSQL/EventLedger only — HARD. Resolve the live DSN and EventLedger/flight-recorder endpoint from the project's standard integration-test config (env var, e.g. HANDSHAKE_TEST_PG_DSN, or the workspace test config used by the E6 backend-wiring MTs and existing integration tests — confirm the exact key from those before coding). Never construct or accept a SQLite path. Never fall back to an in-memory/mock store. If the DSN is missing, fail the proof with `panic!('live PostgreSQL DSN not configured for FEMS interop proof; refusing to run against a fake backend')`.

IN-065-02: Reuse, do not rebuild. Import the WP-011 shell pane registry (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs), command bus (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs), and AccessKit id registry (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs) exactly as MT-031 and MT-041 do. Build the kittest app using MT-041's harness pattern in src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_e7_editor_action_accesskit.rs (same app construction, frame advancement, AccessKit tree query by author_id, and ctx.perform_accesskit_action dispatch). Add the MT-063 FEMS panel and MT-064 propose action on top of that base; do not duplicate any shell or AccessKit glue.

IN-065-03: Bind existing backend APIs only — no backend rewrite. The three backend surfaces this suite exercises are: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/pack (MemoryPack), POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals (create review-gated proposal), and GET /api/flight_recorder (assert FR-EVT-MEM-001). Plus the proposals read path GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals to verify the created row, and the live memory-store read used by FEMS-04 to prove no auto-commit. Confirm the exact route/IPC command names and request/response shapes against the handshake_core API surface and the MT-063/MT-064 contracts before coding. If any of these endpoints does not exist or is shaped differently, record a typed blocker (IN-065-06) and fail the affected proof — do NOT implement or stub the endpoint.

IN-065-04: Provenance assertion. A 'provenance-linked' memory item carries a source/origin reference back to where the memory came from (document id, block id, prior proposal, or ingest source). For FEMS-01, assert at least one rendered item has a non-empty provenance/source field in both the MemoryPack response payload and the rendered panel node. Confirm the exact provenance field name from the MT-063 MemoryPack response schema.

IN-065-05: Correlation, not coincidence. For FEMS-02, the proposal row (from the proposals read API) and the FR-EVT-MEM-001 ledger event must share a common identity (proposal id and/or correlation id). Assert the shared identity explicitly so the proof shows the write and the event are the same logical operation, not two unrelated rows that happened to appear.

IN-065-06: Typed blockers. If a required behavior, panel node, AccessKit id, or backend API is missing (because MT-063/MT-064/MT-041 are not yet complete or shaped differently than expected), do NOT work around it in the test file and do NOT implement product behavior here. Emit a typed blocker: fail the specific proof with a structured message of the form `BLOCKER[kind=missing_api|missing_node|missing_action|schema_mismatch] detail='<what is missing>' source_mt='<MT-063|MT-064|MT-041>'`. This keeps the proof honest (red, not green) and routes the gap back to the owning MT.

IN-065-07: Teardown discipline. Each proof creates its own throwaway workspace_id and seed rows; teardown deletes ONLY the rows this test created (by the captured ids). Never TRUNCATE shared tables and never delete rows the test did not create — other parallel suites and the developer's live data share the PostgreSQL instance.

IN-065-08: Quiet + non-intrusive (HBR-QUIET alignment). The kittest harness runs headless; it must not pop a foreground window, steal focus, or block on interactive input. Drive everything through AccessKit dispatch and frame advancement.

IN-065-09: AccessKit id stability for the swarm path. The FEMS actions follow MT-041's deterministic author_id convention extended to the FEMS pane (e.g. editor.fems.panel-open, editor.fems.memorypack-refresh, editor.fems.propose, editor.fems.proposal-confirm, editor.fems.proposal-cancel). Confirm the exact ids from the MT-063/MT-064/MT-041 deliverables; if an id differs, assert against the actual delivered id and (if it is non-deterministic/random) record a typed blocker against the owning MT per IN-065-06.
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/pack — MemoryPack request from document context; returns provenance-linked memory items (existing handshake_core API; bind only, do not implement). Confirm exact route/IPC name and response schema against MT-063.

POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals — create a review-gated 'Propose to Memory' proposal (existing handshake_core API; bind only). Also read back via GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals to verify the created row. Confirm exact route/IPC name and shape against MT-064.

GET /api/flight_recorder — assert the FR-EVT-MEM-001 event emitted into the live EventLedger, correlated to the created proposal (existing observability API; bind only). Confirm exact route and event-filter shape against the flight-recorder/EventLedger surface.

hbr_obligations: HBR-INT: src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_fems_interop_proofs.rs must pass in CI against a REAL PostgreSQL + EventLedger backend (no SQLite, no mocks); all four proofs green before this MT is DONE.
HBR-SWARM: proof_fems_03 must complete the full FEMS flow purely via stable AccessKit ids (out-of-process agent path); every targeted id must be deterministic and stable across frames.

HBR-VIS: each proof prints its evidence to stdout (AccessKit subtree dump for the rendered MemoryPack items, the proposals read response, and the flight-recorder FR-EVT-MEM-001 excerpt) so a reviewer can read the proof without re-running it.

risks: RISK-065-01: False green on a fake backend — if the harness silently falls back to SQLite, an in-memory store, or a mock when the live PostgreSQL DSN is absent, all four proofs pass without proving anything real.
RISK-065-02: Mocked MemoryPack response — a fixture-constant response could satisfy the render assertion without the live MemoryPack API ever being called, hiding a broken interop edge.

RISK-065-03: Proposal/event correlation gap — FEMS-02 could assert a proposal row AND an FR-EVT-MEM-001 event independently while they belong to two unrelated operations, giving a false correlation guarantee.

RISK-065-04: Review-gate bypass undetected — if FEMS-04 only checks the proposal status field but not the live memory store, a path that BOTH creates a pending proposal AND auto-commits a memory item would still pass.

RISK-065-05: AccessKit id drift — if the FEMS action author_ids are non-deterministic or change between frames, the swarm proof (FEMS-03) could pass once by luck while the real swarm path is unstable.
RISK-065-06: Shared-DB contamination — careless teardown (TRUNCATE or deleting rows not created by the test) corrupts other parallel suites or the developer's live PostgreSQL data.

RISK-065-07: Scope creep into product code — under pressure to make a proof go green, the author implements missing FEMS/backend behavior inside the test file or edits src/, violating the proof-only contract.
minimum_controls: CTRL-065-01: Assert at suite start that the configured store is PostgreSQL (log/assert the DSN scheme is postgres://) and panic if no live DSN is configured; never accept a SQLite path or a mock store. Map to AC-065-01.

CTRL-065-02: For FEMS-01, prove the live API was hit by asserting the response references real PG row ids (not a fixture constant) and that provenance fields are non-empty on >= 1 rendered item. Map to AC-065-02.
CTRL-065-03: For FEMS-02, assert the proposal row and the FR-EVT-MEM-001 event share the same proposal identity / correlation id. Map to AC-065-03 and IN-065-05.
CTRL-065-04: For FEMS-04, assert BOTH the proposal status is pending/review AND a live memory-store query shows no committed memory item created as a side effect. Map to AC-065-05.

CTRL-065-05: For FEMS-03, dispatch every FEMS step via AccessKit author_id only and assert each targeted id is identical across two frame re-queries (stability check); fail if any id contains a random segment or changes. Map to AC-065-04 and IN-065-09.
CTRL-065-06: Teardown deletes only the ids captured by this test; the suite must never call TRUNCATE and must never delete rows it did not create. Map to IN-065-07.

CTRL-065-07: The only file changed is src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_fems_interop_proofs.rs; missing behavior is surfaced as a typed BLOCKER failure (IN-065-06), never implemented in the test and never patched into src/. Map to AC-065-06 and forbidden_paths.
